project: Latent Space Paper Club Podcast Pipeline

stories.title: Scrape Luma Event for Paper Club Session
stories.description: As a user I want to automatically discover the latest Paper Club event from Luma so that I don't have to manually find the paper URL each week.
acceptance_criteria: Script fetches the Luma calendar page at https://lu.ma/ls
Filters events containing 'Paper Club' (case-insensitive) in the title
Selects the most recent past event with timezone-aware datetime comparison
Parses event description to extract paper URLs (arxiv.org or .pdf) and supplementary URLs
arXiv URLs normalized to https://arxiv.org/abs/{id} form
Returns PaperClubEvent dataclass with title, date, event_url, paper_urls, supplementary_urls
If no paper URLs found, returns event with paper_urls=[] and logs WARNING

stories.title: Download Paper PDF
stories.description: As a user I want to automatically download the paper PDF from arXiv or a direct URL so that it can be fed into NotebookLM.
acceptance_criteria: Given arXiv URL, converts to PDF URL and downloads
Given direct PDF URL, downloads the file
Saves PDF to tmp/ with format {arxiv_id}.pdf or {url_hash[:12]}.pdf
Returns pathlib.Path of downloaded PDF
Raises PaperDownloadError on failure
Respects arXiv rate limiting (3-second delay)
For multiple papers, downloads all and returns list[Path]

stories.title: Find and Download YouTube Video Audio
stories.description: As a user I want to find the matching Paper Club video on YouTube and download its audio so that NotebookLM can use the discussion as context.
acceptance_criteria: Two-step discovery: list videos from @LatentSpaceTV, match against event
Matching: title contains 'Paper Club' AND upload/release date within match_window_days
Downloads audio-only using yt-dlp -x --audio-format mp3 --audio-quality 128K
Saves to tmp/{video_id}.mp3
Returns Path of audio or None if no match
Logs WARNING if no match found

stories.title: Download Supplementary Content
stories.description: As a user I want to grab any blog posts or threads linked in the event description so that NotebookLM has additional context.
acceptance_criteria: Fetches text content from non-paper URLs using trafilatura
Saves each as {url_hash[:12]}.txt in tmp/
Returns list[Path] of successfully fetched files
URLs that fail are logged at WARNING and skipped

stories.title: Generate Podcast via NotebookLM
stories.description: As a user I want to feed the paper(s), video audio, and supplementary content into NotebookLM so that it generates a deep-dive podcast episode.
acceptance_criteria: Creates NotebookLM notebook titled 'LSPC: {event_title} ({date})'
Uploads all paper PDFs, video audio, and supplementary text as sources
Calls generate_audio() with configured format, length, and focus prompt
Episode slug = {date_YYYYMMDD}-{sha256(event_url)[:8]}
Downloads MP3 to docs/episodes/{slug}.mp3
Returns Path of downloaded MP3
Raises PodcastGenerationError if NotebookLM fails after retries

stories.title: LLM+TTS Podcast Fallback
stories.description: As a user I want to fall back to an LLM+TTS pipeline so that I still get episodes if notebooklm-py breaks.
acceptance_criteria: Extracts transcript from YouTube captions or Whisper transcription
Generates two-speaker dialogue via litellm with configurable model
Converts dialogue to audio via OpenAI TTS with two voices
Saves MP3 to docs/episodes/{slug}.mp3
Triggered automatically when US-005 raises PodcastGenerationError
Raises FallbackConfigError if OPENAI_API_KEY missing

stories.title: Generate and Update RSS Feed
stories.description: As a user I want to generate a valid podcast RSS feed so that Apple Podcasts can sync new episodes.
acceptance_criteria: Generates RSS 2.0 XML with itunes and atom namespaces
Each item has title, description, pubDate, guid, enclosure, itunes:duration
Feed channel includes title, description, author, itunes:image, atom:link
Feed validates with feedparser (bozo == 0)
Episodes ordered newest first
Writes to docs/feed.xml preserving existing episodes

stories.title: Deduplication and State Tracking
stories.description: As a user I want to track which episodes have been processed so that the same session isn't processed twice.
acceptance_criteria: Maintains processed.json mapping event URLs to processing metadata
Event marked processed only after episode successfully pushed
Checks canonicalized event URL and paper URL set for dedup
Re-processes if live paper URLs differ from stored set
Events with empty paper_urls exit with code 5 (retriable)

stories.title: Pipeline Orchestration
stories.description: As a user I want to run the full pipeline with a single command so that everything happens end-to-end.
acceptance_criteria: python -m src.pipeline runs the full pipeline
Accepts --paper-url, --video-url, --force, --backfill flags
Retries up to max_retries with exponential backoff
Exits with code 2 if video not found (retriable)
Logs all actions to logs/run_{timestamp}.log
No secrets in log output
File lock prevents overlapping runs (exit code 3)

stories.title: GitHub Pages Publishing
stories.description: As a user I want to automatically push new episodes to GitHub Pages so that the RSS feed updates without manual intervention.
acceptance_criteria: Two-commit publish: episode+feed first, then state update
Push failure at step 4 reverts via git reset --hard HEAD~1
Push failure at step 5 allows idempotent retry next run
Pre-commit size check: MP3 < 95MB or re-encode
Credential files, tmp/, logs/, .env in .gitignore

stories.title: Cron Job Setup
stories.description: As a user I want to set up a scheduled cron job on WSL so that the pipeline runs automatically.
acceptance_criteria: cron_setup.sh prints crontab line and prompts for confirmation
Schedule: daily at configurable time with internal day-gating
Uses absolute paths to Python and project directory
Detects existing entry and skips duplicate installation

stories.title: Configuration File
stories.description: As a user I want to configure the pipeline via a YAML file so that I can adjust settings without editing code.
acceptance_criteria: config.yaml contains all settings (luma, youtube, notebooklm, fallback, rss, errors, schedule)
Pipeline loads config at startup into typed dataclass
Missing config.yaml raises FileNotFoundError pointing to config.example.yaml
config.example.yaml committed with all fields documented
Invalid YAML or missing fields raises ConfigError
